title: 70   Laboratory Manual 4. Take any point M 1   on the line   l . Draw the perpendicular to   l   at this point.
subject: Mathematics
activityTitle: 70   Laboratory Manual 4. Take any point M 1   on the line   l . Draw the perpendicular to   l   at this point.
objective:
An alternative method of constructing a
parabola.
Cardboard, white paper, sketch pen,
pencil, compasses, ruler, nails,
thread.
observation:
1. P 1 M 1   = ________   P 1 S = _________
2. P 2 M 2   = ________   P 2 S = _________
3. P 3 M 3   = ________   P 3 S = _________
4. P 4 M 4   = ________   P 4 S = _________
5. P 5 M 5   = ________   P 5 S = _________
6. The distance of the point P 1   from M 1   = The distance of P 1   from _______.
7. The distance between the points P 2   and M 2   = The distance of P 2   from
_______.
The distance of the point _______ from M 3   = The distance of the point P 3
from ______.
8. Distances of the points P 1 , P 2 , P 3   .... from the line   l   are ______ to the distances
of these points from the point S.
9. Therefore, the free hand curve obtained by joining P 1 , P 2 , P 3 , ... is a _______
with directrix _________ and focus _______.
Mathematics   71
10. Distance of the vertex P 4   and S = ______.
11. Distance of the vertex of parabola from the directrix = ______.
discussion:
1. This activity is useful in understanding the terms related to parabola, like
directrix, focus, property of the point on the parabola.
2. Parabolas have applications in Science and Engineering.
